layman_headline: Extreme fear grips the crypto market — patience is the best play right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is deep in 'Extreme Fear' territory at 22/100, and nearly every altcoin is bleeding. Bitcoin is flat while altcoins are dropping 10-30% over the past week. While fear this extreme historically creates buying opportunities, none of the current candidates show strong enough technical setups — most are in confirmed downtrends with bearish chart structures. The best approach is to wait for a clear reversal signal before committing capital.
layman_action: Stay in cash and watch the market. The fear is real but no coin is flashing a clean buy signal yet. Wait for a bounce confirmation before jumping in — catching a falling knife in this environment is risky.
expert_reasoning: Every swing candidate suffers from bearish structure: all are below their major MAs, BTC dominance at 56.1% is squeezing altcoins, and the macro backdrop is risk-off with Fed rate hike fears. DEXE has momentum but RSI at 85 with a -16.7% drop today screams blow-off top reversal. GRASS dropped 32.6% in a week but has negative chart bias and poor risk/reward (0.69x). BONK's chart is strongly bearish (-1.40 score) with a bearish Marubozu confirming continued selling pressure. MON is the least bad option with a neutral chart bias and approaching oversold RSI, but the downtrend structure and unfavorable risk/reward ratio (0.79x) don't justify capital deployment. The Fear & Greed at 22 with an improving trend suggests we're near a bottom, but 'near' can mean days more of pain. Better to wait for confirmation.
risk_factors: US government moving $297M in seized crypto to Coinbase could trigger further sell pressure across the market
BTC dominance at 56.1% and rising means capital continues rotating out of alts — altcoin entries face structural headwinds
No volume breakout signals detected on any candidate — without volume confirmation, any bounce attempt is likely to fail
watch_list: BTC price action around $62,000 support — a breakdown below could trigger another wave of alt selling
Fear & Greed index movement — a sustained climb above 30 would signal improving conditions for entry
MON price approaching $0.019-0.020 support zone with RSI below 30 would create a much cleaner entry setup
market_context: The market is in Extreme Fear (22/100) with BTC flat at $62.7K while alts bleed heavily. High BTC dominance (56.1%) confirms capital is fleeing alts, and upcoming inflation data plus Fed rate hike fears are keeping risk appetite suppressed.
context_banner: Extreme Fear creates opportunity, but without confirmed reversal signals on any candidate, discipline demands patience over premature entries.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 22 (Extreme Fear) with improving trend from 23.9 weekly avg — historically a contrarian buy zone but too early without technical confirmation.
US government moving $297M in seized BTC/ETH to Coinbase Prime creates overhang sell pressure and negative sentiment across the market.
Best candidates (MON 36.85, BONK 33.09) are approaching oversold but haven't reached extreme levels that historically trigger reliable bounces.
Bitcoin slipping on rising July Fed rate hike bets ahead of inflation report — macro headwind suppressing risk appetite for crypto.
No volume breakout signals detected on any candidate — absence of this historically 100% reliable signal argues against forcing an entry.
